constraints: Hugging Face access policies, repository gates, service availability, and rate limits still apply
Large responses and files are locally bounded; full binary downloads remain outside the reviewed public tool surface
human_boundaries: Calling an arbitrary third-party Space can have unknown side effects, inputs, costs, or policies, so only its API contract is exposed in the reviewed matrix.
Creating, deleting, moving, uploading, committing, tagging, branching, and changing repository settings require authenticated ownership and controlled fixtures.
Jobs, scheduled jobs, Inference Endpoints, providers, and other billable compute require explicit financial and execution authorization.
Likes, follows, comments, discussions, and pull-request changes affect public social state and require authenticated confirmation; scripted likes are prohibited.
Login, account recovery, tokens, OAuth grants, passkeys, CAPTCHA, and consent require the user's identity and human authorization.
Members, roles, service accounts, resource groups, network security, audit logs, SCIM, and billing require privileged organization accounts.
Private repositories and gated file downloads require a separately authorized account; AgentWeb never bypasses access controls.
Space duplication, hardware, storage, secrets, variables, restart, logs, and metrics require account access and may incur charges.
Storage Bucket creation, upload, settings, and deletion require authenticated state and controlled test fixtures.
The public Blog and social Posts surfaces do not expose supported exhaustive read APIs and remain visible outside this declared matrix.
coverage.website_parity: All supported anonymous public operations in the declared en-US Hub, Dataset Viewer, research, community, and compute-discovery matrix are browserless. Authenticated, billable, mutation, unsupported editorial, and arbitrary third-party Space execution boundaries remain catalogued outside the matrix.
